meta.title: Charaka Samhita Knowledge Module
meta.description: Key chapters from Charaka Samhita organized by sthana for practical Ayurvedic knowledge

chapters.summary: The origin of Ayurveda and its purpose in achieving long life and health. Describes the Trisutra - the three pillars of Ayurvedic science: cause, symptom, and treatment.
Guidelines on quantity of food, daily routine (Dinacharya), and hygiene practices. Emphasizes eating in proper quantity as the foundation of health and agni maintenance.
Describes Ritucharya - the seasonal regimen for each of the six Ayurvedic seasons. Outlines how diet, sleep, and lifestyle should be adapted season by season to preserve health.
Lists natural body urges that should not be suppressed (like hunger, sleep, urine) and those that must be controlled (like greed and anger). Suppression of natural urges leads to disease.
Describes the three essential desires - life, wealth, and afterlife - and how Ayurveda supports fulfilling them through health. A healthy life enables the pursuit of Dharma, Artha, and Moksha.
Classifies diseases based on their origin - body, mind, and both. Explains the role of three doshas (Vata, Pitta, Kapha) in causing imbalance and the concept of Sama and Vishama dosha states.
Comprehensive chapter on Ahara (food) - its qualities, digestion process, and effect on the body and mind. Explains how food becomes the foundation of life, strength, complexion, and ojas.
Details the twenty-four substances and qualities that constitute the material universe according to Samkhya philosophy. Explains how Panchamahabhuta theory underpins Ayurvedic pharmacology.
Describes the causes, types, and pathogenesis of Jwara (fever) - called the king of diseases. Explains how Vata, Pitta, and Kapha individually and in combination cause different fever patterns.
Covers 20 types of Prameha (urinary and metabolic disorders including diabetes). Details their dosha-based classification, causative factors including sedentary lifestyle and excess sweet intake.

Outlines the four essential limbs of treatment: physician, drug, attendant, and patient. Describes qualities needed in each for successful treatment and introduces the concept of Pariksha (examination).
Explores the metaphysical and physical nature of the human being - the union of Purusha and Prakriti. Discusses the six stages of embryonic development and the role of the soul in the body.
Describes month-by-month fetal development in the womb. Covers the role of maternal diet, emotions, and environment in shaping the child's Prakriti (constitution).
Comprehensive coverage of Rasayana - the science of rejuvenation and anti-aging. Details Amalaki Rasayana, Chyawanprash, and other formulations that restore vitality, memory, and immunity.
Treatment protocols for all types of Jwara. Covers Langhana (fasting/lightening) as the primary approach, followed by herbal formulations like Guduchi and Nimba for each dosha type.
Treatment of Grahani (digestive fire disorders, IBS, malabsorption). Emphasizes Agni restoration through diet, herbs, and Panchakarma. Explains the four types of Grahani based on dominant dosha.
